references.text: De Wilde, WJ & BE Duyfjes. 2012. Revision of Cyclocodon Griff. ex Hook. f. & Thomson (Campanulaceae). Thai Forest Bulletin (Botany)40: 20-25.
Lammers, TG. 2011. Revision of the Malesian species of Lobelia section Rhynchopetalum (Campanulaceae: Lobelioideae). Blumea 56: 218–224
Moeliono, B & P Tuyn. 1960. Campanulaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 6: 107-141
Wang, Q, S-L Zhou & D-Y Hong. 2013. Molecular phylogeny of the platycodonoid group (Campanulaceae s. str.) with special reference to the circumscription of Codonopsis. Taxon 62: 498-504.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 May 2025

species.citation: (Oliv.) W.J.de Wilde & Duyfjes , Thai Forest Bull., Bot. 40 (2012) 22; ≡ Campanumoea axillaris Oliv. , Hooker's Icon. Pl. 18 (1888) t.1775.
species.distribution: China, Japan, Laos, Moluccas, Philippines, Sumatra, Taiwan, Vietnam.

species.citation: (Roxb.) Kurz , Flora 55 (1872) 303 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Thai Forest Bull., Bot. 40 (2012) 23; ≡ Codonopsis lancifolia (Roxb.) Moeliono , Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1960) 120; ≡ Campanumoea lancifolia (Roxb.) Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 587; ≡ Campanula lancifolia Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 96. ssp. lancifolius. Distribution: China, India, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya. Notes: Wet places. 280-1500m. Native. ssp. celebicus (Blume) K.E.Morris & Lammers, Novon 9 (1999) 387; ≡ Cyclocodon celebicus (Blume) D.Y.Hong, Acta Phytotax. Sin. 36 (1998) 109; ≡ Codonopsis lancifolia ssp. celebica (Blume) Moeliono, Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1960) 121; ≡ Campanumoea celebica Blume, Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 727; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 587. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, Lanao del Sur (photos), PANAY. Notes: Forests and shaded ravines, 600-1100m. Native.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Borneo, China, India, Java, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LUZON: Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur (photos), Lanao del Sur (photos), MINDORO, PANAY.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) G.Don , Gen. Hist. 3 (1834) 717 ; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; ≡ Laurentia longiflora (L.) Peterm. , Pflanzenreich (Petermann), 1st Ed. (1845) 444 ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 140; ≡ Isotoma longiflora (L.) C.Presl , Prodr. Monogr. Lobel. (1836) 42; ≡ Lobelia longiflora L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 930.
species.distribution: Caribbean. LUZON: Albay (photos), Laguna (photos), Quezon (photos), MINDORO (photos), SIARGAO.
species.notes: Almost throughout Malesia and throughout the Philippines. Damp places, along ditches and streams, against walls, low elevation.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 June 2026

species.citation: C.E.C.Fisch. , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1928 (1928) 141; Type: Smith 0012 (K, holo), Indonesia. ssp. luzonica (E.Wimm.) Lammers, Blumea 56 (2011) 223; ≡ Lobelia epilobioides var. luzonica E.Wimm., Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (276c) (1968) 882; Type: Clemens 9170 (L, holo; B, iso), Philippines, Luzon, Benquet Subprov., ‘Pauai’ [Paoay], Jan-1915. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet. Notes: Mossy forest among shrubs. Fl. Jan-Jul. 2100m. Native.
Schult. , Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 5 (1819) 50 ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 129. = Lobelia bialata (A.DC.) Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 105 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 588.
species.distribution: Africa, China, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sumatra. LUZON: Mountain Province.

species.citation: (Pers.) Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 588; ≡ Lobelia filiformis var. luzoniensis Pers. , Syn. Pl. (Pers.) 2 (1806) 214. ≠ Lobelia alsinoides auct. non Lam. ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 126.
species.distribution: China, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Singapore, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Taiwan. LUZON: Laguna, NCR, MINDANAO: Davao.
species.notes: Open damp places at low elevation. 0-1000m.

species.citation: Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 3 (1792) 589; ≡ Pratia nummularia (Lam.) Benth. ex Kurz , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 46(2) (1877) 210 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 589. ≠ Lobelia angulata auct. non G.Forst. ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6(1960) 133.
species.distribution: Australia, China, India, Indochina, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, S America, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos), Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur (photos), Misamis Oriental (photos), NEGROS.
species.notes: Moist open or shaded places, in montane forests, along stream banks and forest trails. 600-3300m.

species.citation: Skottsb. , Acta Horti Gothob. 4 (1928) 13; Type: Vanoverbergh 38 (S, lecto; B, FI, GB, L, MO, isolecto), Luzon: Bontoc, 14-Feb-1914. ≠ Lobelia nicotianifolia auct. non B.Heyne ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 588; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 123. ssp. biliranensis Lammers, Blumea 56 (2011) 221; Type: Sulit 5578 (L), Philippines, Biliran, Mt Suiro, northern slope, open ridge, 1230 m, 13-May-1954. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BILIRAN. Notes: Endemic, only known from type (Lammers 2011). Native. ssp. negrosensis Lammers, Blumea 56 (2011) 221; Type: Philippine Plant Inventory 6380 (BRIT, holo), Negros: Negros Occidental, Mt Dumalabdab, Talisay mun., 10°40'N, 123°16'E, hill forest, rocky clay, 1160m, 25-Mar-1992. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. NEGROS, PANAY (photos). Notes: Evergreen rainforest and grassland. Fl. & fr. Mar-Jun. 100m (photos) to 1160-1200m. Native. ssp. philippinensis. = Lobelia nicotianifolia var. mollis Elmer, LPB 9 (1934) 3180; Type: Elmer 22181 (PNH, holo, lost; MO, iso), Luzon: Pampanga, Camp Stotsenburg, Mt Pinatubo, May-1927. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Ifugao (photos), Ilocos Norte, Zambales. Notes: Forest openings. Fl. & fr. Oct-Apr. 500-1600m. Native.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BILIRAN, CAMIGUIN, LUZON: Apayao (photos), Aurora, Benguet, Ifugao (photos), Ilocos Norte, Mountain Province, Zambales, MINDANAO: South Cotabato (photos), NEGROS, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: In Luzon from Ilocos Norte to Aurora to Zambales. Open spots on ridges in the mossy forest, often on grassy mountain slopes and hillsides. 100-1600m.

species.citation: Argent & P.Wilkie , Pl. Ecol. 192(2) (2007) 158; Type: Argent & Reynoso 8998 (PNH, holo; E, K, L, iso), Sibuyan: Mt Giting-giting, Camp 3 above Magdiwang, on ridge leading to Mayo’s Peak, c. 1400m, 24-Aug-1989.
species.notes: Open grassland and elfin forest on ultramafic soils atop a ridge crest. Fl. May-Aug. 1400m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 932 ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 128. = Pratia ovata Elmer , LPB 2 (1909) 593 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 589.
species.distribution: China, India, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Cagayan, Laguna, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Open spots in primary forests, along streams and shady grassy grounds under everwet climatic conditions. Fl. & fr. throughout the year. (?-)1500(-2000)m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 1 September 2023

species.citation: (Wall.) Hook.f. & Thomson , J. Proc. Linn. Soc., Bot. 2 (1857) 26 ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 110; ≡ Campanula carnosa Wall. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 102. = Peracarpa luzonica Rolfe , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1906 (1906) 201 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 586.
species.distribution: China, India, Japan, New Guinea, Philippines, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Nueva Vizcaya, PANAY.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2017

species.citation: (Thunb.) A.DC. , Monogr. Campan. (1830) 143 ; Moeliono, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 115; ≡ Campanula marginata Thunb. , Syst. Veg., 14th Ed. (1784) 211. = Wahlenbergia bivalvis Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 242 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 587.
species.distribution: Australia, China, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, New Zealand, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon (photos), PANAY (photos).
